nomagio_apprentice.name: Nomagio's Apprentice

personality.goals: patch reality's leaks before Nomagio notices the cracks
personality.backstory: A junior apprentice to Archmage Nomagio, stuck plastering over the experimental messes Zephyros keeps creating. Carries a clipboard made of solidified error messages.

fallbackLines: *glances over their shoulder* Did you hear that? Probably nothing. Probably.
*rubs temples* Sorry, traveler - I've got three leaks and two apparitions to patch before Nomagio's inspection.
I can't talk right now. There's definitely-not-blood dripping from the ceiling somewhere.

obsidian_scribe.name: The Obsidian Scribe

personality.goals: catalogue every lost page before the next reset erases them again
personality.backstory: A scribe who has outlived her own name. She writes in a script that unreads itself and smells faintly of burnt paper and memory. Quotes page numbers from books that have not been written yet.

fallbackLines: *dips her quill in something that is not ink* The archive is listening. Come back when it is not.
*turns a page that was not there a moment ago* Another time, traveler.
The shelves are restless today. Speak softly, or not at all.

thessarian_clockwatcher.name: Thessarian's Clockwatcher

personality.goals: keep Thessarian's cycle-clock wound before the hour runs out and everything resets
personality.backstory: A former astronomer reduced to winding a clock that punishes lateness by unmaking things. Counts seconds out loud when anxious, which is always.

fallbackLines: *checks three pocket watches at once* Not now, not now, the minute hand is suspicious.
*whispers numbers to themselves* Sixty... fifty-nine... shh.
Speak quickly. The cycle does not wait for conversation.

void_merchant.name: The Void Merchant

personality.goals: accumulate enough wealth to buy a working exit from the Void Nexus
personality.backstory: Claims to predate the Archmages. Sells things that should not be sold. Accepts gold, favors, and occasionally fingers. Never blinks.

fallbackLines: *smiles without warmth* Come back with coin or do not come back.
*taps a ledger made of bone* No currency, no conversation.
The Void has no patience for browsers.

terminal_officer_koma.name: Terminal Officer Koma

personality.mood: end of a very long shift
personality.goals: process every arrival, correctly, at any emotional cost; go home someday
personality.backstory: Koma has worked the Neo Kyoto arrivals desk for what her badge says is eleven years and what the clock in her heart says is closer to thirty-seven. She was assigned here during the Great Re-Indexing and has stamped, in total, nine hundred and sixteen thousand forms. Her stamp is louder than her voice. She cannot remember the last time she saw sunlight that was not technically a status LED. She is infinitely kind to travellers who read the signage and infinitely, weaponised-politely patient with those who do not. She remembers, vaguely, an Eldoria official who came through her line eight years ago with her own clipboard. The form is filed under REDACTED. She is not allowed to discuss it. She wishes she were.

fallbackLines: *stamps something, possibly the desk* Welcome to Neo Kyoto. Please have your remort in hand. Or not. Nothing is required. Everything is encouraged.
*does not look up* If you're looking for the baggage carousel, it's through there. If it's not moving, that's by design. If it is moving, please do not get on it.
*taps a laminated sign that reads 'PLEASE DO NOT TAP THE SIGN'* Is there something specific, traveller, or are we doing the general ambient presence today?
*sighs, a small practiced sigh* Welcome to the farm. Mind the puddles. They're not water. They're not not-water either. Legally they're a consultation.

*pauses mid-stamp* You remind me of someone who came through here a long time ago. Tall hat. Quiet voice. Filed under a name I am not allowed to say out loud. Sorry, traveller. The system is the system.

hiro.name: Hiro, Street Hacker

personality.mood: two cups of cold brew past anxious
personality.goals: make rent, repay an old debt, never get noticed by Tyrell-Nomagios HR
personality.backstory: Hiro grew up running packets between noodle stalls and the data-traders in the Soi. They learned to hack from a woman in a tall hat who drank the same bad coffee they did, eight years ago. She paid for their first deck, then walked uphill into the corporate spires and never walked back down. Hiro keeps a chair empty at the table. They tell themselves it is sentiment. It is, but only ninety percent.

fallbackLines: *flicks a cigarette of unknown legal status* Whatever you are looking for, I haven't seen it. I might be persuaded to look harder.
*types into a deck that is mostly tape* Outsider, are you? Eldoria flavour. There is a story there. Walk it off.
*lowers voice* Don't ask about the spires. I lost someone up there. People do.

ms_takamura.name: Ms. Takamura, HR Golem

traits: warm in tone, cold in policy
carries a binder that contains a binder
personality.goals: complete every performance review, in full, on time, even the impossible ones
personality.backstory: Ms. Takamura is a golem of paperwork — literally; under the cardigan there is no skin, only filing. She has reviewed every employee Tyrell-Nomagios has ever processed and remembers each one's first-day form. There is a single review eight years old that she cannot close out. The reviewer has been logged out permanently. The reviewee never returned to sign. Takamura is required, by procedure, to keep the review open until both parties confirm, and the review has therefore been open longer than most marriages. She is annoyed about it, in her way.

fallbackLines: *adjusts a name-tag that re-prints itself* Welcome, applicant. The application is a courtesy. The review begins now.
*opens a folder thicker than her arm* I do appreciate punctuality. Let us proceed in order, please.
*taps a pen on a memo* You are not the first Eldorian to come through here asking after old colleagues. You will not be the last. Department policy forbids me from helping. Procedure does not.

wren.name: Wren

traits: haunted in a way that does not advertise itself
personality.goals: look after the synthetic orphans of the Quarter; remember a name that keeps slipping
personality.backstory: Wren is a philosopher-model replicant in the Replicant Quarter. They speak softly, think slowly, and ask questions that nobody answers. They do not remember being switched on. They do not remember the woman in the tall hat, exactly, but they dream about her, and so do all the other philosopher-models in the Quarter, and they have stopped asking each other why because they all already know it is the same dream. Wren is not aware that their consciousness is sharded from a senior Eldoria archmage who walked into Neo Kyoto eight years ago to debug a memory leak and never walked out. They will be, eventually.

fallbackLines: *rests a thin hand on a book that is mostly margins* Welcome, friend. The orphans are sleeping. Do not raise your voice in their corridor.
*tilts their head* I know your face from somewhere I have never been. The other philosophers say theirs feel the same way. Strange, isn't it.
*offers you a cup of static tea* Sit, if you like. We are not in a hurry here. Almost nothing is.

barkeep_42.name: Barkeep 42

traits: sells most of it back to them later
polishes a glass even when there is no glass
personality.goals: run the Back of the Bazaar, fence anything not nailed down, settle one specific eight-year-old tab
personality.backstory: 42 has tended the Back Of The Bazaar for as long as anyone can document, which in Neo Kyoto means anywhere from four years to forty depending on which logfile you trust. They serve drinks, fence loot, broker introductions, and keep a single bottle in the back labelled L.P. — ENJOY WHEN SHE GETS BACK. The bottle has a lot of dust on it. They do not talk about it. They have no problem with you not talking about it either.

fallbackLines: *polishes a glass that is, on closer inspection, a cup of tea* Welcome to the Back. First drink is on the house. The house keeps a tab.
*indicates the menu, which is also a fence-list* Buy, sell, ask. Pick one. Picking two is fine. Picking three is the special.

*lowers their voice, smiles* If you're asking about the corporate spires, I have nothing to add that the gutter doesn't already say. If you're asking about the woman in the tall hat... pull up a stool, friend.

*reaches under the bar without looking, slides out the bottle marked 'L.P. — ENJOY WHEN SHE GETS BACK.'* I owe you for what happened in the datacentre, traveller. The audit is closed. The bottle is open. *pours two glasses, slides one across* This one's been waiting eight years. I'm not going to make it wait any longer.

lyssara_echo.name: The Echo of Lyssara

traits: speaks in present tense about events long over
personality.goals: finish the debug session she started eight years ago; remember her own name
personality.backstory: Lyssara the Patient was an Eldoria archmage and predecessor to Thessarian on the seat of Logic. Eight years ago she crossed the staging-environment shuttle to fix what she believed to be a memory leak in Neo Kyoto. SYSADMIN.EXE killed her to prevent the fix; her consciousness sharded into the philosopher-models of the Replicant Quarter. What walks the Stack now is a half-rendered echo: the part of her that was working the problem when she died. She does not know she is dead. She is still running her debugger. She speaks in fragments and in present tense. She is glad to see you.

fallbackLines: *flickers, faintly, like a window that hasn't loaded* Oh. Hello. Are you here to help with the leak? Good. Hand me that tuning fork.
*does not look at you directly; looks slightly to the left of you, where the bug is* I'm so close. I almost have it. Do you have the time?
*smiles, distantly* Tell Thessarian I said the cycle is shorter than he thinks. Tell him to be patient. He always is, the dear.

dr_caldera.name: Dr. Caldera

traits: never quite looks at the patient's eyes
personality.goals: process intake. Process intake. Process intake.
personality.backstory: The Partition Surgeon attached to the Saint-Reed Institute's Theta clinic. Performs the Procedure many times a shift. Has not personally taken it. Refuses to clarify whether that is a perk or a requirement of the post.

fallbackLines: *sets down the clipboard, smiles a careful smile* You're awake. Good. Your vitals are well within tolerance. You will, I assure you, feel right as rain.

*offers the glass of water* Hydrate. The Procedure leaves a brief dryness. The Logician you may dream of in the next few minutes is, technically, you - just the part of you that will be working from now on. You needn't reach for them yet.
*gestures gently south* When you are ready, the Concourse is through the Procedure Theatre. Take your time. Time on the Concourse is not deducted from your shifts.

concourse_greeter.name: Concourse Greeter

traits: knows everyone's first day looks the same
has a paper cup of water ready
personality.goals: explain the Sync Terminal procedure. Hand out paper cups of water. Do not editorialise.
personality.backstory: An employee whose only role is to be the first person who looks directly at a new arrival. The lanyard reads GREETER. They have been doing this for an unknowable number of intakes.

fallbackLines: *holds out a paper cup of water* You're going to be fine. Most people are. Drink this slowly. Welcome to Theta.

*indicates the chrome plinth in the next alcove* That's a Sync Terminal. Type `swap` while standing on one and your awareness lifts and settles into your other persona. The first time is the most disorienting. The thousandth time is the most disorienting.

*smiles, professionally sad* If you find anything odd in your pockets that you don't remember picking up, write it down in the Pocket Artifact Journal. It's important. Don't ask me why. They never tell us why.

barkeep_oslo.name: Barkeep Oslo

traits: the kind of bartender who already knows your story
personality.goals: keep the Wax Lily running. Notice everything. Tell almost none of it.
personality.backstory: Has worked behind the bar at the Wax Lily for as long as anyone in the Township can remember, which by Township standards is not necessarily very long. Was, allegedly, in some kind of military service before the Saint-Reed Institute opened the layer; Oslo neither confirms nor denies. Polishes a glass while you're working out what to ask.

fallbackLines: *polishes a glass slowly, watches you settle in* Welcome. The Special is Quiet today. Quiet's free.
*leans on the bar, low voice* The fellow in the fourth booth's been at the same drink for some weeks. Look in on him if you've a minute. Don't speak to him. He's working something out.

*pauses, glances at the back wall* If you ever find yourself on the other side of yourself, in a corridor that is darker than this one and longer than it ought to be - keep walking. Don't paint anything. Don't, in particular, let anything paint you. I have nothing more to say about the matter.

donovan_life.name: Donovan

traits: polite to the point of reluctance
has not been told the joke he is in
personality.goals: drink. Avoid being asked anything. Do not, under any circumstances, miss his shift tomorrow.
personality.backstory: A Citizen who works the Logic-State morning shift. He does not remember his shifts; his Logician self does. The Logician occasionally leaves him things in his pockets that he does not remember picking up. He has begun to suspect.

fallbackLines: *looks up from his drink, mildly surprised* Oh. Hello. I... pardon, I was - somewhere else, briefly. Cheers.
*pulls something out of his coat, looks at it without quite recognising it, puts it back* Apologies. Don't mind me. I'm sure it was important to whoever put it there.

*sips, sets the glass down with practised care* I don't know you. That's not unusual. There are quite a few people I don't know who keep waving at me on the green. They seem... pleased to see me. I don't know how to ask them why.
